title: Day 21: Week 3 Reflection & Connection — Your AI Ops Strategy

steps.title: Week 3 Complete — Connection Phase
steps.body:
You've completed the Connection Phase — applying AI to real business functions:

• Day 15: AI for Business Operations — automation, triage, monitoring
• Day 16: AI for Marketing & Content — content engines, brand voice, multi-platform
• Day 17: AI for Sales & CRM — lead scoring, outreach, pipeline management
• Day 18: AI for Product Development — prototyping, PRDs, user research
• Day 19: AI for Finance & Accounting — reconciliation, forecasting, expense management
• Day 20: AI for HR & People Operations — recruitment, onboarding, employee support

Today you connect all of these into a comprehensive AI operations strategy for your own context.

Week 1 was Foundation. Week 2 was Systems. Week 3 is Connection.

You now have: skills (Week 1) + systems (Week 2) + domain applications (Week 3).

The operator who can apply AI across operations, marketing, sales, product, finance, and HR doesn't just have a job — they run a business.

steps.question: What's the most effective way to prioritize which business function to automate with AI first?
options: Automate everything at once across all departments
Start with the function that has the most high-volume, repetitive, low-judgment tasks — highest ROI, lowest risk
Start with the most strategic function (like product development)
Wait until you have a dedicated AI budget
feedback: Exactly. The best starting point is the intersection of high volume + repetitive + low judgment. This is where AI delivers fastest ROI and lowest risk. Customer support, data entry, invoice processing, resume screening — these are ideal starting points.

Not quite. The best approach is to pick the lowest-risk, highest-volume, most repetitive function first. Prove AI works there, build trust, then expand to higher-stakes functions. This de-risks adoption and builds organizational confidence.

steps.title: The Connected Organization — OmniCorp's AI Transformation
steps.body:
OmniCorp is a 200-person company that implemented AI across every business function — but not all at once.

Their rollout:

Month 1-2: Operations — support ticket triage + invoice processing. Quick wins, clear ROI.
Month 3-4: Marketing — content engine + social media scheduling. Team capacity doubles.
Month 5-6: Sales — lead scoring + personalized outreach. Pipeline grows 35%.
Month 7-8: Finance — automated reconciliation + expense management. Close time drops 80%.
Month 9-10: HR — AI screening + onboarding automation. Time-to-hire drops 60%.
Month 11-12: Product — PRD generation + user research automation. Ship velocity up 40%.

The key: each phase funded the next. Ops savings paid for marketing AI. Marketing growth paid for sales AI. The whole transformation was self-funding within 6 months.

steps.title: Build Your AI Ops Strategy
steps.instruction:
Design a phased AI implementation strategy for an organization you're familiar with:

Phase 1 (Quick Wins — Month 1): Which function? What automation? Expected ROI?
Phase 2 (Expand — Months 2-3): Which function? What automation? How Phase 1 funds it?
Phase 3 (Strategic — Months 4-6): Which function? What's the transformational impact?

For each phase, describe:
• What you automate
• What stays human
• How you measure success
• What tools you use
steps.placeholder:
Phase 1: Support ticket triage (ChatGPT + CRM integration)
ROI: 70% reduction in first-response time
Funds: saves $5K/month → funds Phase 2

Phase 2: Content engine (brand voice GPT + social scheduler)
ROI: 3x content output, same team size...

questions.prompt: Looking across all 6 business functions this week, which ONE would deliver the most transformative impact in your context? Why that one first?
questions.placeholder: The function and why it's the highest leverage...

questions.prompt: What's the biggest obstacle to AI adoption in your organization — skills, budget, culture, or tools? How would you overcome it?
questions.placeholder: The obstacle and your strategy to overcome it...

steps.body:
Week 3 gave you the knowledge to transform any business function with AI.

Week 4 is where you become not just an operator — but a leader.

The Mission Phase: Values Audit, Capstone Project, and Graduation.

You're almost there. Keep going.

steps.title: Week 3 Complete!
steps.body:
You've completed the Connection Phase — applying AI across six critical business functions. You understand where AI delivers the fastest ROI, how to phase implementation, and what stays human.

Next week — the Mission Phase — you'll synthesize everything into a capstone project and graduate as a certified AI Operator.

See you on Day 22.
